_comment: Manifest read by installed copies of Whisper Dictate. Updated by release-public.ps1 as part of the dual-publish step. The 'version' field follows SemVer; the 'installer_url' must point at a downloadable .exe asset on a public release of this repo.
release_notes_md:
### Added

- **Pause / Resume hotkey** (`HOTKEY_PAUSE`, default `Ctrl+Alt+H` for "Hold" — `Ctrl+Alt+P` is taken by Claude). Press during recording to pause without losing buffered audio; press again — or press the main record/stop hotkey — to resume into the same buffer. Cancel from paused discards everything (same as cancel-from-recording). To finalize and transcribe from paused, press the main hotkey twice (resume → transcribe). Pause from idle is a no-op + busy beep. New `'paused'` state in tray badge (slate dot), recording overlay (`❚❚ Paused (12s)`), and heartbeat schema. New `'pause'` and `'resume'` event keys in the SOUNDS config (defaults: `DeviceDisconnect` for pause, `SystemAsterisk` for resume) — configurable in Settings → Sounds. The audio stream stays open across pauses (no cold-start cost on resume; mic remains active during pause — documented behaviour).
- **`WHISPERDICTATE_PROFILE` env var** for parallel dev/production isolation. When set (e.g. `dev`), the user data dir, single-instance mutex, AppUserModelID, and window/tray title all gain the suffix so a development checkout and a production install can run side by side without trampling each other's settings or hotkey registrations. New `dev.ps1` launcher script sets the env var and spawns the venv-installed `pythonw.exe`. Hotkeys still need to differ across the two — example shifted defaults documented in `dev.ps1` output and `MAINTAINERS.md`.

- **Daily update check + one-click installer.** The running app polls a publicly-readable manifest at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oscarnorberg/whisper-dictate-public/main/latest.json` ~once per day (with random jitter on first launch and ±1 hr on subsequent). When a newer version is available, an in-app toast appears in the bottom-right overlay slot with four buttons: `Update now` (downloads the installer to `%TEMP%`, verifies SHA256 against the manifest, runs it with `/SUPPRESSMSGBOXES /CLOSEAPPLICATIONS /RESTARTAPPLICATIONS`, exits the app cleanly so the installer's AppMutex/CloseApplications path takes over), `What's new` (displays release notes), `Remind tomorrow` (24-hour snooze), and `Skip this version` (exact-version-string skip; later versions still notify). The toast hides when an active recording overlay needs the slot and re-shows when the engine returns to idle. Two new config keys (both hot-reloadable, both in user.py): `UPDATE_CHECK_ENABLED` (bool, default `True`) and `UPDATE_MANIFEST_URL` (default the raw.githubusercontent URL). New Settings → Behavior section: "Check for updates daily" toggle + "Last checked: ..." timestamp + "Check now" button. New `update_state.json` and `update_history.jsonl` files in `%USERPROFILE%\WhisperDictate\` (per profile).
- **`release-public.ps1`** — new dual-publish automation. Run after the internal-repo `gh release create` to mirror the .exe to the public update channel and bump `latest.json`. See `MAINTAINERS.md` for the updated 11-step release process and the threat-model documentation for the auto-update mechanism.

### Changed

- **Default `PASTE_STRATEGY` reverted to `'clipboard'`** (was `'type'` in v0.2.2). Type mode caused chat apps' autolinkers (Teams, Slack) to fire mid-stream on long messages — the receiving app processed the per-keystroke `SendInput` burst in render-batches, and matched URL-like substrings before the rest of the text arrived. Result: `like. So` would render as `[like.So](http://like.So)` because the autolinker saw `like.So` before the space-S landed. Clipboard delivers the entire string in one `WM_PASTE` event so the autolinker fires exactly once over the final text. The v0.2.0 verify-retry plus a new ~50 ms post-verify settle (gives the OS time to broadcast `WM_CLIPBOARDUPDATE` to receiving apps) handles the original "fast back-to-back wrong-paste" race that motivated the type-mode default. Type mode remains available via `PASTE_STRATEGY='type'` in `user.py` for users who hit specific clipboard-manager / RDP issues.
